title: budget/--estimate/undecided tri-state
manager: The honesty layer for verification: a budget on how hard the checker will try, an `--estimate` of the cost before you commit to it, and a basic true/false/undecided tri-state result — because a checker that can't finish should say "undecided," not hang and not lie. This is what makes the safety checker usable in practice, and it sets the precedent that FSL's verification always tells you when it doesn't know rather than pretending it does.
cs: verification budget + `--estimate` + true/false/undecided tri-state; undecided is a first-class result; the honesty posture the whole proofs era inherits.
